title: anagallis arvensis f. carnea
intro: Anagallis arvensis f. carnea, also known as red pimpernel, is a flowering plant species belonging to the primrose family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which contribute to its health benefits such as wound healing and treatment of skin conditions. Red pimpernel has been used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and ointments, often in combination with other herbs, to take advantage of its medicinal properties. While its effectiveness has not been extensively scientifically studied, anagallis arvensis f. carnea has been used in traditional folk medicine for various health benefits and medicinal purposes.
